creator: rfg@tristatelogic.com
text:
Attempt to build /usr/ports/x11/yelp based on current ports tree fails.

I am marking this PR as priority=high because several other ports depend
upon yelp, I believe.

Log attached:

========================================================================
# make

 yelp is using libxul19 for gecko support, but you can
 change that by defining WITH_GECKO to the following values:

   libxul19

Please run the gnomelogalyzer, available from
"http://www.freebsd.org/gnome/gnomelogalyzer.sh", which will diagnose the
problem and suggest a solution. If - and only if - the gnomelogalyzer cannot
solve the problem, report the build failure to the FreeBSD GNOME team at
gnome@FreeBSD.org, and attach (a)
"/usr/ports/x11/yelp/work/yelp-2.30.2/config.log", (b) the output of the
failed make command, and (c) the gnomelogalyzer output. Also, it might be a
good idea to provide an overview of all packages installed on your system
(i.e. an `ls /var/db/pkg`). Put your attachment up on any website,
copy-and-paste into http://freebsd-gnome.pastebin.com, or use send-pr(1) with
the attachment. Try to avoid sending any attachments to the mailing list
(gnome@FreeBSD.org), because attachments sent to FreeBSD mailing lists are
usually discarded by the mailing list software.
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/x11/yelp.
========================================================================

Fix: 

Dunno.  My hope is that the port maintainer will tell me how to fix this.
How-To-Repeat: 
cd /usr/ports/x11/yelp
make clean
make
